askbob_helper_pattern.overview: The askBob helper function is the standard way to invoke Bob CLI from Jenkins pipeline stages. It encapsulates best practices for calling Bob, handling output, and managing errors.

helper_signature.description: The askBob helper is defined in the Jenkinsfile and provides a consistent interface for all Bob invocations across pipeline stages.

when_to_use.rule: ALWAYS use the askBob helper instead of directly calling bob CLI
when_to_use.rationale: - Ensures consistent container usage (container('bob')) - Standardizes error handling across all stages - Makes pipeline code more readable and maintainable - Centralizes Bob CLI invocation logic for easier updates

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.description: The prompt text to send to Bob (can be multi-line)

best_practices.practice: Use triple-quoted strings for multi-line prompts
Include context like diffs, logs, or file contents
Be specific about what analysis you want

parameter.name: mode
parameter.description: Optional Bob chat mode slug to use (defaults to general mode if not specified)
parameter.selection_guide: See 7_mode_selection_guide.xml for choosing the right mode

return_value.description: Returns Bob's analysis as a String
return_value.usage: Capture the return value to process, display, or archive the analysis

pattern.name: basic_invocation
pattern.description: Simple Bob invocation with console output

pattern.name: with_diff_content
with_output_archiving
with_error_handling
pattern.description: Resilient invocation that continues pipeline on Bob errors

pattern.name: with_console_banners
pattern.description: Add clear visual markers in console output

anti_pattern.name: inline_bob_call
anti_pattern.description: DON'T call bob CLI directly without the helper
anti_pattern.good: // GOOD: Use the helper def analysis = askBob("Review this", 'solution-code-reviewer')

anti_pattern.name: not_capturing_output
anti_pattern.rationale: The helper returns Bob's analysis as a string - capture it to display, process, or archive

anti_pattern.name: missing_container
anti_pattern.description: DON'T call bob CLI directly outside container('bob')
anti_pattern.bad: // BAD: bob isn't in the default (build-tools) container sh "bob --chat-mode solution-code-reviewer -p 'review this'"
anti_pattern.good: // GOOD: askBob enters container('bob') for you def analysis = askBob("review this", 'solution-code-reviewer')

issue.name: bob_not_found
issue.solution: Always use askBob helper which ensures container('bob')

issue.name: analysis_not_displayed
issue.symptom: Bob runs but output not visible in console
issue.cause: Not capturing or echoing the return value
issue.solution: - Capture return value: def analysis = askBob(...) - Echo it: echo analysis - Or save to file: writeFile file: 'output.txt', text: analysis

issue.name: special_chars_in_prompt
issue.symptom: Prompt gets corrupted or Bob fails
issue.solution: - Use triple-quoted strings for multi-line prompts - The helper handles temp file creation automatically - No need to manually escape special characters
